stem: A user uploads a 12 MB file from their laptop to a cloud-storage server.
model_answer: The file is split into smaller packets, each carrying a small piece of the data plus a header containing the source IP, the destination IP, a sequence number and a checksum. Routers forward each packet independently, possibly along different paths. At the destination, packets are reassembled in sequence-number order and any missing or corrupted ones are re-requested.
review_notes: Teacher-marked. Award marks for: split into packets, header content (addresses + sequence), independent routing, reassembly at the destination.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Explain how packet switching is used to send the file across the internet.

mark_points.text: the file is split into multiple smaller packets
each packet has source/destination IPs and a sequence number (and/or checksum)
packets are routed independently and may take different paths
packets are reassembled in order at the destination (and missing ones requested again)
